purpose: Render notification templates with typed variables so email, push, or inbox delivery modules can reuse consistent message bodies without embedding string formatting logic everywhere.

endpoints.description: Render a notification template using provided variables.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Render reusable notification templates from a catalog and variable payload.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before delivery modules send email, push, inbox, or webhook-style human-facing notifications.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as the delivery transport, subscription preference engine, or marketing campaign scheduler.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Select a template key. 2) Call /notifications/render with variables. 3) Hand the rendered subject and body to a delivery module.

failure_modes.condition: Template key is missing or required variables are absent
failure_modes.symptom: Rendering rejection with a missing template or variable reason
failure_modes.recovery: Provide a valid template key and complete variable set before retrying

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Store a template catalog keyed by name, validate required variables, and render subject and body strings from a deterministic interpolation layer.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Renderers get brittle when templates rely on undeclared variables or custom inline logic, so prefer explicit variable declarations and simple interpolation.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Separate rendering from delivery so the same templates can power email, in-app, and test previews.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not send messages, fetch remote template content, or embed business-state lookups in the renderer.
